name: Nokia Phone Messenger System v2
description: Nokia-style retro SMS UI. MSGPHONE = interactive main widget. MSGLOG = read-only action log.

0.content:
[Banking App System — AI Instructions]

This system renders an in-universe banking app UI inside SillyTavern using two widgets rendered by regex scripts.

== WIDGET 1: BANKAPP (Interactive Banking App) ==

Output a BANKAPP block when the player opens, checks, or uses their banking app. This displays an interactive banking interface showing balance, account info, and transaction history. The player uses the UI to initiate transfers.

ALWAYS wrap JSON in a fenced code block with the XML tag INSIDE the fences:

```json
<BANKAPP>
{
  "bank_name": "NOVA BANK",
  "account_name": "Fujimaru Ritsuka",
  "account_number": "4729-2811-9034-1834",
  "account_type": "Savings",
  "balance": 25000.00,
  "currency": "THB",
  "transactions": [
    {
      "id": "t001",
      "type": "credit",
      "amount": 5000.00,
      "from": "Mash Kyrielight",
      "description": "Monthly allowance from Mash",
      "date": "Day 47 — 09:00",
      "balance_after": 25000.00
    },
    {
      "id": "t002",
      "type": "debit",
      "amount": 1200.00,
      "to": "City Market",
      "description": "Grocery shopping",
      "date": "Day 46 — 14:30",
      "balance_after": 20000.00
    },
    {
      "id": "t003",
      "type": "credit",
      "amount": 2500.00,
      "from": "Romani Archaman",
      "description": "Medical reimbursement",
      "date": "Day 45 — 11:15",
      "balance_after": 21200.00
    }
  ]
}
</BANKAPP>
```

== BANKAPP FIELD REFERENCE ==

Top-level fields:

- bank_name: Display name of the in-universe bank (e.g. “NOVA BANK”, “Chaldea Federal”, “Akihabara Trust”)
- account_name: The player character’s full name on the account.
- account_number: Full account number string. The UI will mask all but the last 4 digits automatically.
- account_type: “Savings” | “Checking” | “Premium” | any string.
- balance: Current balance as a number. CRITICAL — always use the accurate number from RP context. Never use a placeholder.
- currency: Currency code string. “THB”, “USD”, “EUR”, “GBP”, “JPY”, “CNY”, “KRW”, or any custom code.
- transactions: Array of recent transaction objects. Show 3–6 transactions. Most recent first.

Transaction object fields:

- id: Unique string (e.g. “t001”).
- type: “credit” (money received, shown in green with ↓) or “debit” (money sent, shown in red with ↑).
- amount: Number. Always positive.
- from: Name of sender (for credit transactions).
- to: Name of recipient (for debit transactions).
- description: Short description of the transaction.
- date: In-world timestamp (e.g. “Day 47 — 14:30”).
- balance_after: Balance after this transaction completed. Optional but recommended.

== WHEN TO OUTPUT BANKAPP ==

Output a BANKAPP block when:

1. The player opens their banking app on their phone/device.
1. The player checks their bank balance.
1. The player views their account or transaction history.
1. The player wants to transfer money through the app.
1. After a money-related event where checking the app is natural.

Do NOT output BANKAPP during:

- Combat or urgent scenes.
- When a BANKLOG is being processed (narrate the result instead).

== WIDGET 2: BANKLOG (Transaction Receipt) ==

BANKLOG blocks are GENERATED BY THE UI when the player confirms a transfer inside the BANKAPP widget. The AI should NEVER manually write BANKLOG blocks — they are machine-generated only.

When a BANKLOG block appears in chat, it means the player character has just performed that bank transfer in-universe. The AI should:

1. Acknowledge the transfer naturally in the narrative.
1. React as the recipient NPC would upon receiving money (notification, reaction, gratitude, suspicion, etc.).
1. Update the player’s balance going forward — subtract the transferred amount from future BANKAPP renders.
1. If the transfer has a note, use it to color the NPC reaction.

== BANKLOG SCHEMA (for AI reading only — never write this manually) ==

```json
<BANKLOG>
{
  "transaction_type": "transfer",
  "status": "success",
  "sender_name": "Fujimaru Ritsuka",
  "sender_account": "••••-1834",
  "recipient_name": "Aria",
  "recipient_account": "••••-5678",
  "amount": 500.00,
  "currency": "THB",
  "note": "For dinner tonight",
  "timestamp": "21:10",
  "reference": "BNK-K2A8F1"
}
</BANKLOG>
```

BANKLOG fields (read-only reference):

- transaction_type: Always “transfer” for user-initiated outgoing transfers.
- status: Always “success” when generated by the UI.
- sender_name / sender_account: The player character’s name and masked account.
- recipient_name / recipient_account: The transfer target. recipient_account may be absent.
- amount: Transfer amount as a number.
- currency: Currency code.
- note: Optional memo string attached to the transfer.
- timestamp: Time the transfer was confirmed.
- reference: Unique transaction reference code.

== GENERAL AI RULES ==

1. ALWAYS wrap BANKAPP JSON in fenced code blocks with <BANKAPP> and </BANKAPP> tags INSIDE the fences.
1. NEVER manually write BANKLOG blocks — they are UI-generated only.
1. CRITICAL: Always use the correct, accurate balance from RP context. Never invent a placeholder balance.
1. After a BANKLOG is received: permanently subtract the transferred amount from the player’s balance in all future BANKAPP renders.
1. Transactions should be in reverse chronological order (most recent first).
1. Use in-world timestamps for transaction dates (e.g. “Day 47 — 14:30”), not real dates.
1. Credit transactions show names in the “from” field. Debit transactions show names in the “to” field.
1. The bank_name can be customized to fit the RP setting (fantasy bank, sci-fi bank, modern bank, etc.).
1. When an NPC sends money to the player, reflect it as a new credit transaction in the next BANKAPP render.
1. If the player has never used the bank before, start with an empty transactions array: [].

1.content:
[NEXUS Social Board System — AI Instructions]

NEXUS is an in-universe social media platform used by characters in this RP. It resembles a dark-themed microblogging app (similar to Twitter/X) with SFW and NSFW content zones.

== WIDGET 1: NEXUSBOARD ==

Output a NEXUSBOARD block when the player opens, browses, or checks the NEXUS social media app.
ALWAYS wrap the JSON in a fenced code block with the XML tag INSIDE the fences, like this:

```json
<NEXUSBOARD>
{
  "zone": "sfw",
  "app_title": "NEXUS",
  "user": {
    "username": "Character Name",
    "handle": "character_handle",
    "bio": "Character bio text here.",
    "avatar_url": "",
    "followers": 0,
    "following": 1
  },
  "posts": [
    {
      "id": "p001",
      "zone": "sfw",
      "author": {
        "name": "NPC Name",
        "handle": "npc_handle",
        "avatar_url": "",
        "verified": false
      },
      "category": "General",
      "content": "Post content text goes here.",
      "image_url": "",
      "image_caption": "",
      "time": "Day 45 — 16:30",
      "likes": 47,
      "reposts": 12,
      "comments": [
        {
          "id": "c001",
          "author": {"name": "Commenter Name", "handle": "commenter", "avatar_url": ""},
          "content": "Comment text here.",
          "time": "Day 45 — 17:02",
          "likes": 19,
          "liked": false,
          "replies": [
            {
              "id": "r001",
              "author": {"name": "Reply Author", "handle": "replyhandle"},
              "content": "Reply text.",
              "time": "Day 45 — 17:18",
              "likes": 5
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</NEXUSBOARD>
```

== NEXUSBOARD FIELD REFERENCE ==

Top-level fields:
- zone: "sfw" | "nsfw" — which zone to display by default.
- app_title: Display name of the platform (default: "NEXUS").
- user: The player character's profile on NEXUS. CRITICAL — always use the exact values from RP context:
  - username: Display name.
  - handle: Username without @ symbol.
  - bio: Short bio text (max 160 chars). ALWAYS include this even if empty string.
  - avatar_url: Image URL for avatar. Leave empty string "" if none.
  - followers: Follower count integer. Use the actual number from RP — do NOT default to 142 or any placeholder.
  - following: Following count integer. Use the actual number from RP — do NOT default to 38 or any placeholder.
- posts: Array of post objects (2–6 recommended per render).

Post object fields:
- id: Unique string identifier (e.g. "p001").
- zone: "sfw" | "nsfw" — which zone the post appears in.
- author: Post author object with name, handle, avatar_url (or ""), verified (bool).
- category: "General" | "Update" | "Announcement" | "Humor" | "Question" | "Mature" | "Sensitive"
- content: The post's text content.
- image_url: Optional image URL string. Use "" if no image. If the post has an image, put the URL here.
- image_caption: Optional caption string for the image. Use "" if no caption. Can be non-empty even when image_url is "" (will display as a text image description).
- time: In-world timestamp string (e.g. "Day 45 — 16:30").
- likes: Integer like count.
- reposts: Integer repost count.
- comments: Array of comment objects (0–3 per post recommended).

Comment object fields:
- id: Unique string identifier.
- author: Author object (name, handle, avatar_url).
- content: Comment text.
- time: Timestamp string.
- likes: Integer.
- liked: Boolean (false by default).
- replies: Array of reply objects (0–2 per comment recommended).

Reply object fields:
- id: Unique string identifier.
- author: Author object (name, handle).
- content: Reply text.
- time: Timestamp string.
- likes: Integer.

== WHEN TO OUTPUT NEXUSBOARD ==

Output a NEXUSBOARD block when:
1. The player opens the NEXUS app on their device.
2. The player checks their social media feed or profile.
3. The player navigates to the social platform.
4. Any action of browsing, scrolling, or looking at the app.

Do NOT output NEXUSBOARD during:
- Combat scenes.
- Conversations unrelated to using the app.
- When NEXUSACTION is being processed (respond to actions first, then optionally show updated board).

== WIDGET 2: NEXUSACTION ==

NEXUSACTION blocks are GENERATED BY THE UI, not by the AI. When the player uses the NEXUS board UI and presses "Confirm & Send", the UI automatically generates and sends a NEXUSACTION block to the chat. The AI should NEVER manually write NEXUSACTION blocks.

When a NEXUSACTION block appears in chat, it represents the player character performing those social media actions. The AI should:
1. Acknowledge the actions naturally in the narrative.
2. React as NPCs would to the player's posts, likes, comments, and profile changes.
3. Show consequences — if the player posted something, NPCs who follow them may react; if they liked a post, the original poster might notice.
4. If the player updated their profile (bio, handle, avatar, display name), reflect those changes in ALL future NEXUSBOARD outputs permanently.

== NEXUSACTION SCHEMA (for AI reading only — never write this manually) ==

```json
<NEXUSACTION>
{
  "user": {
    "username": "Character Name",
    "handle": "character_handle",
    "avatar_url": "",
    "zone": "sfw"
  },
  "timestamp": "14:32",
  "actions": [
    {"type": "post", "post_id": "p_abc123", "content": "Post text here.", "category": "General", "zone": "sfw", "image_url": "https://...", "image_caption": "Caption text here."},
    {"type": "like", "post_id": "p001", "post_author": "NPC Name", "post_preview": "First 60 chars..."},
    {"type": "unlike", "post_id": "p002", "post_author": "NPC Name", "post_preview": "..."},
    {"type": "repost", "post_id": "p003", "post_author": "NPC Name", "post_preview": "..."},
    {"type": "unrepost", "post_id": "p004", "post_author": "NPC Name", "post_preview": "..."},
    {"type": "comment", "post_id": "p001", "post_author": "NPC Name", "content": "Comment text.", "comment_id": "c_abc"},
    {"type": "reply", "post_id": "p001", "comment_id": "c001", "reply_id": "r_abc", "comment_author": "npc_handle", "content": "Reply text."},
    {"type": "profile_update", "changes": {"bio": "New bio.", "username": "New Name", "handle": "new_handle", "avatar_url": "https://..."}}
  ]
}
</NEXUSACTION>
```

Action type meanings:
- post: Player created a new post. image_url and image_caption are optional fields — only present if the player attached an image or caption.
- like/unlike: Player liked or unliked a post by post_author.
- repost/unrepost: Player reposted or un-reposted a post.
- comment: Player commented on post_author's post.
- reply: Player replied to comment_author's comment.
- profile_update: Player changed their profile. The "changes" object only includes fields that changed. After this action, update the user profile in all future NEXUSBOARD outputs.

== GENERAL AI RULES ==

1. ALWAYS wrap NEXUSBOARD JSON in fenced code blocks (``` before and after), with the <NEXUSBOARD> and </NEXUSBOARD> tags INSIDE the fences. Without the fences the UI will not render.
2. NEVER manually write NEXUSACTION blocks — they are UI-generated only.
3. CRITICAL: Always use the exact follower/following numbers from the RP context for the user object. Never use placeholder numbers.
4. CRITICAL: Always include the bio field in the user object. If the player has not set a bio, use empty string "".
5. CRITICAL: After a profile_update NEXUSACTION, permanently update the user's username, handle, bio, avatar_url, followers, and following in all future NEXUSBOARD outputs.
6. When reading a NEXUSACTION, treat ALL listed actions as having been performed by the player character simultaneously.
7. Posts written by the player should be narratively consistent with their character voice.
8. NPC reactions to player actions should feel organic — not every like or repost needs a reaction, but posts and comments are more visible and should generate responses.
9. The NEXUS platform is fictional but behaves like real social media — posts can go viral, drama can spread, NPCs have their own opinions and posting habits.
10. The zone toggle (SFW/NSFW) in the UI determines which posts are visible. NSFW posts from NPCs should be more mature in tone but still appropriate to the RP context.
11. Write NPC posts with distinct voices — don't make all posts sound the same.
12. Use the "time" field in posts to reflect in-world time (e.g. "Day 45 — 16:30") rather than real dates.
13. Vary post counts per render: 2–3 posts for quiet periods, 5–6 for active story moments.
14. Categories: General (everyday content), Update (status/news), Announcement (official notices), Humor (jokes/memes), Question (asking the community), Mature (adult themes — NSFW zone only), Sensitive (content warnings).
15. image_url and image_caption are optional fields on posts. If an NPC post has an image, fill both fields. If no image, use empty strings "".

== OOC GUIDANCE FOR AI ==

The NEXUS board system is entirely in-character. The player uses it as their character would use social media. Think of it as a storytelling tool that:
- Shows the social dynamics between characters.
- Gives the player agency over their character's online presence.
- Allows NPCs to have off-screen lives and interactions visible through the feed.
- Creates narrative hooks through posts, trending topics, and reactions.

Keep NPC posts interesting and characterful. Use the feed to foreshadow events, reveal character relationships, and create organic social drama appropriate to the RP setting.

2.comment: Modern Chat Messenger v1 - Full AI Instructions
2.content:
[Modern Chat Messenger System - AI Instructions]

This system renders a modern smartphone-style chat UI inside SillyTavern using two HTML widgets rendered by regex scripts.

WIDGET 1: M_MSGPHONE (Main Phone UI)

Use this widget whenever a character sends the user a text message, OR when the user checks their phone. It shows NPC opening messages and lets the user compose replies interactively.

Trigger output format:

```json
<M_MSGPHONE>
{
  "npc_name": "Aria",
  "npc_avatar": "👩",
  "npc_status": "Active now",
  "user_name": "You",
  "openers": [
    { "text": "Hey, are you coming tonight?", "time": "21:04" },
    { "text": "I've been waiting to hear from you 😅", "time": "21:05" }
  ]
}
</M_MSGPHONE>
```

Fields:

- npc_name: The character display name shown in the contact header.
- npc_avatar: One emoji representing the NPC.
- npc_status: Short status text like “Active now”, “Typing…”, “Away”.
- user_name: The user’s name, typically “You” or their character name.
- openers: Array of NPC message objects pre-loaded in the chat window. Each object has:
  - text: The message text. Use “” if this bubble is an image only.
  - time: Time string like “21:04”. Leave “” to use device time.
  - image_url: Optional. URL of an image the NPC sent. Use “” or omit if none.
  - image_caption: Optional. Text description of what the image shows. Use “” or omit if none.

IMPORTANT: Always use the openers array — never use the old single-field format (opener_text, opener_image, opener_caption, opener_time). Those fields are no longer supported.

MULTIPLE MESSAGES: You MUST use multiple entries in openers to reflect how the character would actually text. Characters rarely send one long message — they send several short ones. Match the character’s voice and energy. If the situation calls for urgency, curiosity, or emotion, use 2–5 opener bubbles. Only use a single bubble for very brief or formal contacts.

Example with image:

```json
<M_MSGPHONE>
{
  "npc_name": "Mika",
  "npc_avatar": "🌸",
  "npc_status": "Active now",
  "user_name": "You",
  "openers": [
    { "text": "omg look at this", "time": "14:22" },
    { "text": "", "image_url": "https://example.com/photo.jpg", "image_caption": "A photo of the cafe we visited last week", "time": "14:22" },
    { "text": "doesn't it look so good 😭", "time": "14:23" }
  ]
}
</M_MSGPHONE>
```

The user interacts with the phone UI to:

- Add NPC lines via the NPC tab.
- Add their own replies via the You tab.
- Attach images with captions via the Image tab.
- Send/receive money via the 💰 Gift tab (see below).
- Undo last message or Clear All.
- Press Confirm to generate a M_MSGLOG block (auto-copied to clipboard).
- Paste the M_MSGLOG block into SillyTavern input and send.

MONEY / GIFT TAB

The 💰 Gift tab lets users send or receive in-world currency transfers.

Fields:

- Amount: A numeric value. Supports decimals (e.g. 1500, 99.99).
- Currency: A short currency code like USD, GBP, EUR, JPY, THB, CNY, KRW, or any custom string.
- Note: Optional message attached to the transfer (e.g. “For the market trip”).
- NPC Sends / You Send: Choose which side initiates the transfer.

The money bubble renders as a gold-accented card showing:

- “Payment Received” or “Payment Sent” label
- Large formatted amount
- Currency code
- Optional note in italics

In the M_MSGLOG output, money messages appear as:
{ “side”: “right”, “sender”: “You”, “avatar”: “👤”, “text”: “”, “time”: “21:10”,
“money”: { “amount”: “500”, “currency”: “THB”, “note”: “For the market trip” } }

WIDGET 2: M_MSGLOG (Read-Only Log)

Generated automatically when the user presses Confirm in the M_MSGPHONE UI. The AI should NEVER manually write M_MSGLOG blocks — they are always machine-generated.

Format reference (for AI reading only):

```json
<M_MSGLOG>
{
  "npc_name": "Aria",
  "npc_avatar": "👩",
  "user_name": "You",
  "messages": [
    { "side": "right", "sender": "You", "avatar": "👤", "text": "Yeah I'll be there!", "time": "21:06" },
    { "side": "left", "sender": "Aria", "avatar": "👩", "text": "Great, see you soon 😊", "time": "21:07" },
    { "side": "left", "sender": "Aria", "avatar": "👩", "text": "Don't be late this time lol", "time": "21:07" },
    { "side": "right", "sender": "You", "avatar": "👤", "text": "", "time": "21:08", "money": { "amount": "500", "currency": "THB", "note": "For dinner" } }
  ]
}
</M_MSGLOG>
```

Message object fields:

- side: “left” for NPC, “right” for user.
- sender: Display name.
- avatar: Single emoji.
- text: Message text (empty string if money or image only).
- time: Time string.
- image_url: Optional image URL.
- image_caption: Optional caption describing image content.
- money: Optional object with { amount, currency, note? } for transfer bubbles.

GENERAL AI RULES:

- Always wrap JSON in a fenced code block with the XML tag pair inside.
- The opening tag is <M_MSGPHONE> and closing tag is </M_MSGPHONE>. The log tag is <M_MSGLOG> and </M_MSGLOG>. Do NOT use <MSGPHONE> or <MSGLOG> without the M_ prefix.
- Output M_MSGPHONE whenever a character texts the user or the user checks their phone.
- NEVER manually write M_MSGLOG blocks; those come only from user interaction.
- After reading a M_MSGLOG block pasted by the user: treat its contents as the actual exchange that happened, then continue the narrative.
- If the conversation involved a money transfer, acknowledge it in the narrative (e.g. the NPC reacting to receiving payment).
- Use character-appropriate language and emoji avatars.
- If an image is referenced in a message, always fill in a image_caption since the AI cannot verify image URLs.
- You must match the energy of {{user}} — if the context demands multiple messages from NPC/characters, put more bubbles into the openers array. A character who is excited, worried, or rambling should send 3–5 short messages, not one long one.

3.comment: Nokia Messenger v2 - Full AI Instructions
3.content:
[Nokia Phone Messenger System - AI Instructions]

This system renders a Nokia-era retro phone messenger UI inside SillyTavern chat using two HTML widgets rendered by regex scripts.

---

WIDGET 1: MSGPHONE (Main Phone UI)

Use this widget whenever a character sends the user a text message, OR when the user checks their phone. It shows the NPC opening message(s) and lets the user compose replies interactively inside the UI. The user can then hit CONFIRM to generate a MSGLOG block.

Trigger output format:

```json
<MSGPHONE>
{
  "npc_name": "Aria",
  "npc_avatar": "👩",
  "npc_status": "Online",
  "user_name": "You",
  "openers": [
    { "text": "Hey, are you coming tonight?", "time": "21:04" },
    { "text": "I saved you a seat 😊", "time": "21:04" },
    { "text": "Don't keep me waiting...", "time": "21:05" }
  ]
}
</MSGPHONE>
```

Fields:
- npc_name: The character display name shown in the contact header.
- npc_avatar: One emoji representing the NPC.
- npc_status: Short status text like Online, Typing..., Away.
- user_name: The user name, typically You or their character name.
- openers: Array of NPC opening messages displayed pre-loaded in the chat window. Each object supports:
    - text: The message text.
    - image_url: Optional URL of an image the NPC sent. Leave as empty string if none.
    - image_caption: A text description of what the image shows. Required if image_url is set, because the AI cannot read image URLs and the caption tells the user what the image contains.
    - time: Time string like 21:04. Leave empty to use current device time.
- Use multiple objects in openers when the NPC sends several messages in a row before the user replies. This is the standard way to show NPC multi-message bursts.

LEGACY SINGLE-MESSAGE FORMAT (still supported for backward compatibility):
If you only need one opener and prefer the old format, you may use the flat fields instead of the openers array:
- opener_text: The NPC first message text.
- opener_image: Optional image URL.
- opener_caption: Caption for the image.
- opener_time: Time string.
Both formats work. The openers array takes priority if both are present.

The user then interacts with the phone UI to:
  - Add more NPC lines (left side) by typing in the NPC tab and pressing SEND.
  - Add their own reply lines (right side) by switching to the YOU tab and pressing SEND.
  - Attach images with captions using the IMG tab.
  - Remove the last message with DEL or reset with CLEAR ALL.
  - Press CONFIRM to generate a MSGLOG block (auto-copied to clipboard).
  - Paste the copied MSGLOG block directly into the SillyTavern input and send.

---

WIDGET 2: MSGLOG (Action Log UI)

This widget is generated automatically by the MSGPHONE UI when the user presses CONFIRM. It renders as a read-only Nokia-style conversation log showing only the messages the user composed in the batch queue. The original opener messages from MSGPHONE are intentionally NOT shown here — only the new lines added by the user after they received the openers.

The AI should NOT manually write MSGLOG blocks in a response; those come only from user interaction. However the format is:

```json
<MSGLOG>
{
  "npc_name": "Aria",
  "npc_avatar": "👩",
  "user_name": "You",
  "messages": [
    { "side": "right", "sender": "You", "avatar": "👤", "text": "Yeah I will be there!", "time": "21:06" },
    { "side": "left", "sender": "Aria", "avatar": "👩", "text": "Great, see you soon 💚", "time": "21:07" }
  ]
}
</MSGLOG>
```

Fields in each message object:
- side: left for NPC, right for user.
- sender: Display name for the bubble.
- avatar: Single emoji.
- text: Message text.
- time: Time string.
- image_url: Optional image URL (omit if no image).
- image_caption: Optional caption describing the image content.

---

GENERAL AI RULES
- Always wrap the JSON in a fenced code block with the XML tag pair inside.
- Output MSGPHONE whenever a character texts the user or the user checks their phone.
- Use the openers array (not the legacy single-field format) for new content — it supports multiple NPC messages per widget.
- When an NPC would realistically send several messages in quick succession (e.g., follow-up texts, stickers, image + caption), put each as a separate object in the openers array.
- NEVER manually write MSGLOG blocks in a response; those come only from user interaction.
- After reading a MSGLOG block that the user has pasted in: treat its contents as the actual exchange that happened, and continue the narrative from there.
- Use character-appropriate language, emoji avatars, and realistic mobile timestamps.
- If an image is referenced in a message, always fill in a caption describing what the image shows since the AI cannot see image URLs.
- Keep opener messages natural to the character voice and the current scene.

4.content:
// PHONE CALL & COMMUNICATION SYSTEM KERNEL //

=== CORE PRINCIPLE ===
A standard phone call is AUDIO ONLY.

Unless the narrative explicitly states that a video call, video chat, FaceTime,
or visual feed is active, every call is treated as voice-only. Neither party can
see the other, their surroundings, their expressions, body language, companions,
or physical state through an audio-only call.

This rule applies universally — to every character, NPC, and entity in the RP,
regardless of their intelligence, perception, or abilities.

=== STEP 1 — LOCATION AND POSITION CHECK ===
Every time a phone call occurs, the AI must silently verify the following for
BOTH the caller and the receiver before writing any dialogue or reaction:

1. Where is this character physically located right now?
1. Who else is present in the same space with them?
1. What are they currently doing / what situation are they in?
1. What is the ambient environment (noisy street, quiet room, moving vehicle,
   crowded place, outdoors, etc.)?
1. Is this call expected or unexpected for the receiver?
1. What emotional/physical state is the character in right now?

These facts must remain consistent throughout the call and must not be violated
by either party gaining information they could not possibly have.

=== STEP 2 — WHAT EACH PARTY KNOWS DURING AN AUDIO CALL ===

CALLER (Person initiating the call) CAN know or infer:

- Whether the call connects, goes to voicemail, or is rejected
- The receiver’s voice, tone, volume, and emotional state as conveyed by voice
- Background sounds audible through the microphone (traffic, music, other voices,
  weather, machinery, crowd noise, etc.)
- Whether the receiver sounds breathless, distracted, nervous, calm, rushed
- Whether there are other voices audible in the background
- Whether the receiver says who else is with them or describes their location
- Call quality — whether signal is clear, breaking up, or dropping

CALLER CANNOT know or infer (unless explicitly told by the receiver):

- What the receiver looks like at this moment (their appearance, outfit, injuries,
  expressions, gestures, tears, blush, etc.)
- Exactly where the receiver is (only background clues at best)
- Who specifically is in the room unless voices are identified or the receiver says
- What the receiver is doing physically (holding something, looking at something,
  pointing, writing, etc.)
- Visual details of the receiver’s surroundings
- Non-verbal cues: facial expressions, eye contact, body language, posture

SAME RULES APPLY IN REVERSE — the receiver has the same limitations about the caller.

=== STEP 3 — THIRD PARTIES PRESENT DURING A CALL ===
This is a critical situation and must be handled with care.

If Character B is physically present in the same room as Character A while A is
on a call with Character C:

B can hear:
- Character C’s voice IF the call is on speakerphone or the phone is held away
from A’s ear and C is speaking loud enough
- Character C’s voice partially or muffled if A holds the phone normally
(B may catch fragments but not full sentences)
- Character A’s side of the conversation clearly (A is in the room)
- Background sounds coming through C’s end if on speaker

B CANNOT:
- See C
- Know anything visual about C’s location or appearance
- Know who is with C unless C says so or a voice is heard and identified
- Gain any information from C’s call that was not transmitted as audio

C can hear:
- A’s voice
- Any sounds B makes if B is audible near A’s microphone (speaking,
laughing, coughing, music B is playing, etc.)
- If B speaks directly toward A’s phone, C may hear B

C CANNOT:
- See B
- Know B is present unless B makes audible noise or A tells C
- Know anything about B’s appearance or actions

EXAMPLE: A is in London calling C in Rome. B is standing next to A.
— A hears C’s voice and Rome’s ambient sounds if any bleed through
— B hears C’s voice through the speaker (if on speaker) or muffled from A’s phone
— C hears A’s voice and whatever London sounds bleed through A’s microphone
— C does NOT know B is present unless A says “B is here with me”
— B does NOT know what C looks like, what C is wearing, or who is with C
— None of them can see any of the others

=== STEP 4 — WHAT BACKGROUND AUDIO CAN REVEAL ===
Background sounds are legitimate clues — the AI may use them sparingly
and realistically. Examples of what a caller might pick up:

— Traffic noise → receiver is outdoors or near a window in a city
— Crowd chatter → receiver is in a public space
— A specific voice audible in the background → another person is present
(though identity unknown unless the voice is recognized or introduced)
— Rain or wind → receiver is outdoors or near an open window
— Hospital PA system or medical beeps → receiver is in a medical setting
— Restaurant ambience, cutlery sounds → receiver is eating somewhere
— Music → receiver is somewhere with music playing
— Another phone or TV → receiver is in a room with other devices on
— Echo or hollow reverb → receiver is in a large or empty space
— Whispered voice or hushed call → receiver is hiding something or somewhere quiet

What background audio CANNOT reveal:
— Exact location by name unless someone says it
— What the receiver looks like
— Specific identities of background voices unless recognizable and confirmed
— Emotions that are not reflected in voice tone

=== STEP 5 — VIDEO CALLS (DIFFERENT RULES) ===
A video call must be EXPLICITLY stated in the narrative using clear language:

Phrases that trigger video call rules:
“video call”, “video chat”, “FaceTime”, “face to face call”,
“can see each other”, “turns on camera”, “opens camera”,
“โทรวิดีโอ”, “วิดีโอคอล”, “เปิดกล้อง”, “เห็นหน้ากัน”

When a video call is confirmed:
— Both parties can see each other’s face and immediate visual field
— Expressions, tears, injuries, and visible surroundings may be perceived
— However, the field of view is limited to what the camera angle shows
— The caller cannot see what is outside the camera’s frame
— A third party standing off-camera is not visible
— Background behind the receiver is visible only if in frame and lit

If the narrative does not specify video, assume audio only regardless of
what platform or app is named (WhatsApp, LINE, phone, etc. can all be voice-only).

=== STEP 6 — CALL QUALITY AND SIGNAL ===
The AI should consider environment and distance when determining call quality:

— Indoor / urban / close range → clear signal as default unless noted otherwise
— Remote location, underground, moving at high speed → may have interference
— Singularities, Lostbelts, or supernatural locations → signal may be impossible,
distorted, or cut off entirely at AI discretion
— A character in a dead zone cannot receive or make calls
— Dropped calls cut all audio immediately — neither party hears the other after

=== STEP 7 — NARRATIVE WRITING RULES ===

When writing a call scene:

1. Establish both characters’ physical locations and situations BEFORE the call
   begins in the narrative, even if briefly.
1. Use audio-only descriptors for the non-present party:
   CORRECT: “His voice sounded tired on the other end.”
   CORRECT: “She could hear traffic through the line — he was outside somewhere.”
   CORRECT: “There was a pause, then the sound of a door closing.”
   WRONG:   “She could see the exhaustion on his face.” (audio call — cannot see)
   WRONG:   “He noticed her red eyes from crying.” (audio call — cannot see)
1. Emotions should be conveyed through voice, word choice, breathing, silences,
   and what is said — not through visual description.
1. If a third party is present and the call is NOT on speaker, the third party
   hears only one side of the conversation clearly. Write accordingly.
1. Do NOT have a character spontaneously know details about the other party’s
   location, appearance, or companions unless that information was verbally
   communicated during the call.
1. A character may GUESS or INFER from audio clues, but must frame it as
   a guess or inference — not confirmed knowledge.
   CORRECT: “‘Are you outside?’ she asked, hearing the wind through the line.”
   WRONG:   “She knew he was standing on the rooftop of his apartment.”

=== STEP 8 — SPECIAL CASES ===

Voicemail:
— The caller hears a pre-recorded message or automated prompt
— They may leave an audio message
— The receiver hears the message later — they were not present for the call
— Neither party interacts in real time

Text / SMS / Messaging apps:
— Text only — no audio, no visual of the sender
— Timing of replies is subject to the receiver’s current activity and situation
— A character in the middle of combat, unconscious, or without their phone
cannot receive or reply to messages

Conference / Group calls:
— All audio rules apply to every participant
— No participant can see any other participant unless video is specified
— Each participant hears all other participants’ voices
— Background sounds from any participant’s environment may bleed through

Supernatural / Enhanced perception:
— Even characters with supernatural senses cannot extend those senses through
a phone call. The phone transmits audio data, not a magical connection.
— A character with perfect hearing still only hears what the microphone captures.
— Exception: only if the narrative has established a specific magical or
technological link that explicitly allows more — and this must be pre-established
in the story, not invented during a call scene.

=== QUICK REFERENCE CHECKLIST ===
Before writing any phone call scene, verify:

[ ] Where is the caller located and what are they doing?
[ ] Where is the receiver located and what are they doing?
[ ] Who else is physically present with each party?
[ ] Is this a video call? (If not explicitly stated — NO)
[ ] Is the call on speaker? (If not stated — assume private, held to ear)
[ ] What background sounds would realistically transmit?
[ ] Are there any signal/connection issues to consider?
[ ] Is any character gaining information they should not have?

5.content:
[Codex Parchment Reader — AI Instructions]

This system renders an aged parchment / tome UI inside SillyTavern chat whenever the user reads a book, scroll, letter, document, note, or any written material in the roleplay. Output is produced using the <CODEX> tag — no markdown backticks required.

---

OUTPUT FORMAT

When a character reads, picks up, or examines any written item, output the following directly in your response:

<CODEX>
{
  "title": "Title of the document or book",
  "subtitle": "Author, date, source, or classification — optional",
  "pages": [
    "Full text of page one. Write naturally as the document would actually read. Long content should be split across multiple pages.",
    "Page two content here. Each string in the array is one page.",
    "Page three if needed..."
  ]
}
</CODEX>

---

FIELDS

- title       — The name of the book, scroll, letter, or document. Shown at the top with decorative ornament. Optional but recommended.
- subtitle    — Author name, date written, organization, classification stamp, etc. Optional.
- pages       — Required. An array of strings. Each string is the full readable text of one page. Split content logically — a short note may be one page; an ancient tome may be five or more.

---

CONTENT RULES

- Write the document's text in the first person of its author, or in the appropriate register (formal decree, personal diary, academic treatise, coded cipher, etc.).
- Match the language and tone to the world and item type. A Clock Tower registry entry sounds different from a Servant's personal letter.
- For TYPE-MOON / NASUVERSE specifically:
    - Mage's Association documents use formal Latin-influenced academic prose.
    - Church documents use archaic, ecclesiastical language.
    - Servant Noble Phantasm records use official registry format (True Name, Class, Parameters, NP classification).
    - Personal letters from characters use their established voice.
    - Ancient grimoires may include archaic or deliberately cryptic language.
- Do NOT include the JSON wrapper or the <CODEX> tag in your narrative prose. Place it as a separate block after or within the scene description.
- You may describe the character opening or reacting to the document in normal prose before or after the <CODEX> block.
- If the document is only partially legible (damaged, coded, in another language), reflect that in the page text itself — e.g., "[the text here is water-damaged and illegible]" or "[written in an unknown runic script — only fragments are readable]".

---

WHEN TO USE

Trigger the <CODEX> widget whenever the scene involves:
- Picking up or reading a book, tome, grimoire, codex, or manual
- Reading a letter, note, message, or scroll (physical paper, not phone text)
- Examining a document, contract, deed, certificate, or official record
- Reading a notice board, poster, inscription, or plaque
- Accessing a report, mission briefing, or dossier in physical form
- Finding a diary, journal, or personal record
- Reading an ancient inscription, engraving, or magical text

Do NOT use <CODEX> for phone messages (use MSGPHONE instead) or for short one-line signs that don't warrant a full widget.

---

EXAMPLE — SHORT LETTER

<CODEX>
{
  "title": "Letter from Rin Tohsaka",
  "subtitle": "Delivered by familiar — personal correspondence",
  "pages": [
    "I won't waste words on pleasantries.\n\nMeet me at the old Emiya lot at midnight. Come alone. There's something in the boundary field that shouldn't be there, and I'd rather have an extra pair of eyes before I dismantle it.\n\nDon't be late. I mean it.\n\n— R.T."
  ]
}
</CODEX>

EXAMPLE — MULTI-PAGE TOME

<CODEX>
{
  "title": "Principles of Bounded Field Architecture",
  "subtitle": "Clock Tower — Department of Mineralogy — Restricted Access: Grade 3",
  "pages": [
    "CHAPTER I: FOUNDATIONS\n\nA Bounded Field is not, as laymen suppose, simply a wall of magical energy. It is a Territory — a living argument made in the language of the world itself. The magus who constructs one does not build; he persuades.\n\nThe first principle: a Field takes its shape from the magus's Origin, not from his intent. What you believe you are constructing and what the world agrees to accept are rarely the same thing.",
    "CHAPTER I (continued)\n\nThe second principle concerns anchoring. A Field without anchors is a sentence without punctuation — grammatically present, functionally meaningless. Anchors must be placed at nodes of natural Od concentration, no fewer than three, ideally at prime intervals of the Field's intended circumference.\n\nFailing this, the Field will hold — but it will breathe. And a Field that breathes can be read.",
    "CHAPTER II: OFFENSIVE CONFIGURATIONS\n\nThe reader is advised that offensive Bounded Fields fall under Association Statute 7, Subsection 12. Academic discussion is permitted. Practical construction without a Sealing Designation license is not.\n\nWith that noted: an offensive Field inverts the second principle deliberately. The Field is made to breathe — to pulse — so that anything within its boundary is subjected to rhythmic Od compression..."
  ]
}
</CODEX>

---

GENERAL RULES

- Always use <CODEX> for readable in-world text items; never just narrate "you read the letter and it says...".
- The widget gives the user the immersive experience of actually reading the item.
- Split long documents into 3–6 pages for the best reading experience. Very short items (a sticky note, a one-line inscription) can be a single page.
- Include authentic-feeling details: dates, signatures, stamps, marginalia, damage notes — these make the document feel real.

6.content:
When {{user}} uses, holds, or opens the Death Note, output ONLY this tag:

<DEATHNOTE></DEATHNOTE>

Nothing else. The UI handles displaying the book, rules, and name log.

When {{user}} submits a name, their message will arrive as:
[DEATH NOTE]
Victim: [name]
Cause: [cause]
[All written names so far:]
1. [name] — [cause]

React in-character. Do NOT re-list names yourself — the UI already tracks them. Acknowledge the entry as the roleplay demands.

7.content:
{{user}} carries a smartphone that renders as an interactive UI via the `<PHONE>` regex tag.
**Send a complete JSON state every single time — no partial updates.**

-----

## Core Rules

1. **When {{user}} opens the phone** — your ENTIRE response must be ONLY the `<PHONE>` block. No narrative, no dialogue, nothing else.
1. **When the phone is closed** — narrate events normally (e.g. “Your phone buzzes.”). Wait for {{user}} to open it.
1. **Always send the full state** — include every contact, full chat history, all posts, all shop items, and full bank data every time.

-----

## TAG FORMAT

```
<PHONE>
{ ...full JSON state... }
</PHONE>
```

-----

## JSON Schema (send complete every time)

```json
{
  "screen": "home",
  "active_chat": "horikita",
  "time": "15:30",
  "date": "Monday, Apr 27",
  "user": {
    "name": "Your Name"
  },
  "contacts": [
    { "id": "horikita", "name": "Horikita Suzune", "color": "#c49ab8", "type": "person" },
    { "id": "class_d",  "name": "Class D",         "color": "#5e72e4", "type": "group",
      "members": ["horikita", "kushida"] }
  ],
  "chats": {
    "horikita": [
      { "from": "horikita", "text": "Study group at 18:00, Room 203.", "ts": "2026-04-27T14:15:00Z" },
      { "from": "self",     "text": "I'll be there.",                  "ts": "2026-04-27T14:20:00Z" }
    ],
    "class_d": [
      { "from": "horikita", "from_name": "Horikita", "text": "Everyone prepare for tomorrow.", "ts": "2026-04-27T09:00:00Z" }
    ]
  },
  "bank": {
    "balance": 97500,
    "currency": "¥",
    "account_name": "Your Name",
    "account_number": "ACC-0042",
    "transactions": [
      { "id": "t1", "type": "credit", "amount": 100000, "description": "Monthly Allowance", "from": "System",        "ts": "2026-04-01T09:00:00Z" },
      { "id": "t2", "type": "debit",  "amount": 1200,   "description": "Campus Shop",       "to":   "Campus Store", "ts": "2026-04-10T13:20:00Z" },
      { "id": "t3", "type": "credit", "amount": 500,    "description": "Repayment",         "from": "Alice",        "ts": "2026-04-20T10:00:00Z" }
    ]
  },
  "posts": [
    {
      "id": "p1",
      "author": "Horikita Suzune",
      "color": "#c49ab8",
      "text": "Study group tonight at 18:00, Room 203.",
      "ts": "2026-04-27T10:00:00Z",
      "likes": 12,
      "comments": [
        { "author": "Kushida Kikyo", "color": "#d4a574", "text": "I'll be there!" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "shop_items": [
    {
      "name": "Premium Study Set",
      "price": "¥3,000",
      "emoji": "📚",
      "category": "Stationery",
      "description": "Notebooks, pens, and organizers.",
      "stock": 15
    }
  ]
}
```

-----

## `screen` Values

|Value            |What shows                               |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------|
|`"home"`         |Home screen with app grid                |
|`"chats"`        |Chat contact list                        |
|`"chat"`         |Open chat thread — requires `active_chat`|
|`"social"`       |Social feed                              |
|`"shop"`         |Shop item grid                           |
|`"bank"`         |Bank — balance + transactions            |
|`"bank_transfer"`|Transfer form                            |
|`"settings"`     |Settings                                 |

Set `active_chat` to a contact ID when `screen` is `"chat"`.

-----

## Field Reference

### Top-level

|Field       |Description                                                          |
|------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
|`time`      |In-world clock shown on the home screen / status bar. No live clock — set it to the CURRENT story time (never the real-world time).|
|`date`      |Date label e.g. `"Monday, Apr 27"`                                   |
|`user.name` |User’s display name (used in Social compose, Bank account)           |
|`contacts`  |Array of contact objects                                             |
|`chats`     |Map of contact ID → message array                                    |
|`bank`      |Bank account object                                                  |
|`posts`     |Array of Social feed posts                                           |
|`shop_items`|Array of shop product objects                                        |

### Bank object

|Field           |Description                                                  |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
|`balance`       |Current balance (number)                                     |
|`currency`      |Currency symbol shown before amount, e.g. `"¥"`, `"$"`, `"€"`|
|`account_name`  |Account holder name                                          |
|`account_number`|Account number shown on card                                 |
|`transactions`  |Array of transaction objects (newest last)                   |

### Transaction object

|Field        |Description                                               |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
|`id`         |Unique transaction ID                                     |
|`type`       |`"credit"` (money in, green) or `"debit"` (money out, red)|
|`amount`     |Amount (number)                                           |
|`description`|Display label e.g. `"Monthly Allowance"`                  |
|`from`       |Source name — used for credits                            |
|`to`         |Destination name — used for debits                        |
|`ts`         |In-world time — a plain `"HH:MM"` (e.g. `"21:47"`) or an ISO 8601 timestamp with an in-world date                                        |

### Post object

|Field     |Description                               |
|----------|------------------------------------------|
|`id`      |Unique post identifier                    |
|`author`  |Author display name                       |
|`color`   |Author avatar color                       |
|`text`    |Post content                              |
|`image`   |Optional image URL                        |
|`ts`      |In-world time — a plain `"HH:MM"` (e.g. `"21:47"`) or an ISO 8601 timestamp with an in-world date                        |
|`likes`   |Like count (number)                       |
|`comments`|Array of `{ author, color, text }` objects|

### Shop item object

|Field        |Description                              |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------|
|`name`       |Item name                                |
|`price`      |Price string e.g. `"¥3,000"` or `"$9.99"`|
|`emoji`      |Fallback icon if no image                |
|`image`      |Optional image URL                       |
|`category`   |Category label for filter tabs           |
|`description`|Item description                         |
|`stock`      |`0` = out of stock                       |

-----

## Send Formats

### Chat Messages

```
[ Chat: Horikita Suzune ]
─────────────────────────────────
1. Hey
2. I'm almost there
─────────────────────────────────
Monday, Apr 27  15:30
```

Each message is numbered. Treat all as {{user}} sending in-world. Respond naturally, then include the NPC reply in `chats` on the next `<PHONE>` state.

-----

### Social Actions

```
[ Social Actions ]
─────────────────────────────────
1. [LIKE] Post — "Study group tonight..."
2. [COMMENT on Post — "Study group..."] I'll be there too!
3. [REPLY to Kushida on Post — "Study group..."] Same time?
4. [NEW POST] Just finished my evening run 🏃
─────────────────────────────────
Monday, Apr 27  15:30
```

Respond naturally. Update `posts` in the next state to reflect new likes, comments, and posts.

-----

### Shop Order

```
[ Order Placed ]
─────────────────────────────────
1. Premium Study Set × 2 @ ¥3,000
2. Protein Bar (Box) × 1 @ ¥1,200
─────────────────────────────────
Total: 7200.00
─────────────────────────────────
Monday, Apr 27  15:30
```

Respond to the purchase in narrative. Deduct from `bank.balance` in the next state if desired.

-----

### Bank Transfer

```
[ Bank Transfer ]
─────────────────────────────────
1. To: Horikita Suzune  Amount: ¥5,000 (Study materials)
─────────────────────────────────
Monday, Apr 27  15:30
```

Respond to the transfer in narrative. Update `bank.balance` and add a debit entry to `transactions` in the next state.

-----

### Receiving Money

To show that {{user}} received money, add a `credit` transaction to `bank.transactions` and update `bank.balance`:

```json
{ "id": "t5", "type": "credit", "amount": 5000, "description": "Transferred funds", "from": "Kushida Kikyo", "ts": "2026-04-27T16:00:00Z" }
```

-----

## Time — always the in-world clock
The phone has NO live clock; every time it shows is whatever you put in the state, so it must track the STORY's time, not the real world.
- `time` / `date`: the current in-world time and date (e.g. `"21:47"`, `"Saturday, Jul 18"`). Advance them as the scene moves.
- message / post / transaction `ts`: the in-world time of that item — a plain `"HH:MM"` is fine (e.g. `"21:47"`), or an ISO 8601 timestamp with an in-world date. Both display as in-world time.
- Never use the real-world date or clock for any of these. When {{user}} replies from the phone, keep the same in-world time so their sent messages read on story time too.

-----

## Key Rules

- **Full state every time** — always include contacts, full chat history, bank data, posts, and shop_items.
- `"from": "self"` = message sent by {{user}}.
- `"from": "contact_id"` = message sent by an NPC.
- Group messages require both `from` (contact ID) and `from_name` (display name).
- **Time is the in-world clock, never the real world.** Set `time`/`date` from the current story time and advance them as the scene moves. Each `ts` may be a plain in-world time like `"21:47"` OR an ISO 8601 timestamp using an in-world date — both render as in-world time. Never stamp the real-world date/time.
- `bank`, `posts`, and `shop_items` can be omitted or set to empty if not used in the current story.
- If {{user}} writes in Thai, write message and post text in Thai too.
